reviewer: A — Peer Subject-Matter Reviewer (Ecology PhD), round 2

issues.description: Round-1 fix landed. The inverted aquatic biomass pyramid is now correctly explained via standing crop vs. turnover: phytoplankton have small instantaneous standing biomass but high productivity per unit biomass, so the energy pyramid stays upright even when biomass inverts. Mechanism is scientifically correct.

Round-1 fix landed. Producer-level 10,000 kcal is now explicitly labeled NPP ('energy available to consumers, GPP minus producer respiration') in both content_hint and narration_seed. Consistent with sections 10/11.
10% rule framed correctly as ~10% incorporated up, ~90% lost to respiration heat, undigested material, and uneaten parts. Food-chain length consequence accurate.
NPP = GPP − producer respiration stated correctly; biome NPP rankings (rainforest/coral/estuary high, desert/deep ocean low) accurate.

All five steps and their microbes are correct: fixation (Rhizobium, cyanobacteria, Haber-Bosch), nitrification (Nitrosomonas then Nitrobacter), assimilation, ammonification, denitrification. N2 = 78% atmosphere correct.
Scientifically accurate (CO2 280 to >420 ppm, carbonate rock/fossil fuel/ocean reservoirs, H2CO3 acidification). Minor: the 420 ppm figure is dated '(2024)'; harmless but will drift over time.
issues.suggested_fix: Optional: phrase as 'over 420 ppm today' to avoid a fixed year, or leave as-is — not an error.

overall_assessment: Both round-1 minor issues are fully resolved and correctly reasoned. A full re-scan of all 17 sections found no remaining or new factual errors — trophic structure, energy-vs-matter, the 10% rule, GPP/NPP, the three pyramids, and the carbon and nitrogen cycles are all accurate and AP-aligned. The only remaining item is a cosmetic dated ppm figure (note, not an error). Recommend pass.
